During the Cold War, leadership was shared between two major global powers. In the United States, several presidents held the title of head of state. These included Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush. On the other side, the Soviet Union was led by leaders such as Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, Andropov, and Chernenko. These individuals shaped policies, managed tensions, and influenced the course of international relations throughout the period.
